title: Boat Rental in Cadiz: Best Boats and Prices Guide
html:
For the best boats to charter in Cadiz, set off from the marina at Puerto América and pick from open speedboats, family motor cruisers or crewed yachts, with full days roughly from EUR 350 to EUR 2,500. BoatHire24 lists verified boats across this Atlantic stretch of the Costa de la Luz.

Where to set off in Cadiz

Cadiz sits on a narrow Atlantic peninsula, so departure points spread around the bay and along the open Costa de la Luz coast. Most charters leave from one of a handful of well-run marinas.

  • Puerto América: the city marina, closest to the old town and the easiest meeting point.
  • Puerto Sherry, at El Puerto de Santa María, one of the larger berthing harbours on the bay.
  • Rota, across the bay, handy for the western beaches.
  • Sancti Petri, the channel near Chiclana, popular for calmer sheltered water.
  • Chipiona, further north towards the Guadalquivir, good for longer coastal runs.

Which boat to rent in Cadiz?

The right boat depends on your group size and whether you want speed, shade or an overnight cabin. Cadiz suits everything from nimble day boats to crewed cruisers, and the lineup below covers the common choices.

  1. Open speedboat: best for couples and quick coastal hops, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 350 a day.
  2. Family motor cruiser: comfortable shaded seating for a day out, up to 8 guests, from EUR 600. The Fairline is a good example of this motor yacht class, with a cabin and a swim platform.
  3. Sailing yacht: ideal for steady Atlantic breezes and a slower pace, up to 8 guests, from EUR 450.
  4. Catamaran: stable and roomy for groups and parties, up to 12 guests, from EUR 900.
  5. Crewed motor yacht: for a premium skippered day, up to 10 guests, from EUR 1,500.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Cadiz?

A shared seat starts low while a private crewed yacht runs into four figures, so the price depends entirely on boat size and whether you bring a skipper. The bands below give a realistic picture for a day on the water, and you can hire a boat across most of these tiers through one marketplace.

  • A shared group tour seat is usually from EUR 35 to EUR 60 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half day is roughly EUR 250 to EUR 400.
  • A catamaran for a full day typically runs EUR 900 to EUR 1,600.
  • A crewed yacht for a full day commonly starts around EUR 1,500 and rises with size.

When is the best season to charter in Cadiz?

The high season runs June, July, August and September, when warm settled weather and long daylight make the Atlantic coast most reliable. Cadiz weather is shaped by two famous local winds, so timing matters more here than on the sheltered Mediterranean.

The Levante blows hard from the east and can churn the bay, while the milder Poniente from the west tends to bring calmer, clearer water; ask your operator which is forecast before you book a long crossing.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Cadiz or list your own. Spain follows the country principle that day charters with a skipper need no licence from guests, while bareboat hire requires a recognised qualification, and the operator confirms the exact requirements for each boat.

How does booking a boat in Cadiz work?

You choose a boat and date, send a request, and the owner or operator confirms before any money is taken. Each listing shows capacity, what is included and whether a skipper comes as standard, so there are few surprises on the day. A typical trip lasts between four and eight hours, and the sheltered bay sits only a couple of nautical miles from the open Gulf of Cadiz.

At a glance

  • Price band: from EUR 350 to EUR 2,500 per day.
  • Peak months: June to September.
  • Trip length: 4 to 8 hours.
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests on larger boats.
  • Currency: EUR.
  • Year: 2026.

Questions about boat rental in Cadiz

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Cadiz?

No licence is needed when you book a skippered charter, as the professional skipper holds the qualification and runs the boat for you. For bareboat hire you must show a recognised certificate, and the operator confirms what counts.

How many people can come aboard?

Capacity depends on the boat, ranging from around 6 guests on a small speedboat to about 12 on a larger catamaran. Each listing states its legal maximum clearly, so you can match the boat to your group before booking.

What is there to see on the water near Cadiz?

Charters typically cruise the wide bay, the long city beaches such as La Caleta, and the open Costa de la Luz coastline towards Rota and Sancti Petri. Calmer days allow swim stops in clear, sheltered Atlantic water.

How long is a typical charter day?

Most day charters run between four and eight hours, with half days popular for families and full days suited to longer coastal cruising. Departure times flex with the wind forecast and the season you choose.

A day on the Atlantic off Cadiz is genuinely memorable, from quiet morning swims to long golden evenings, and the choice of verified boats keeps it straightforward to book. If you own a boat on this coast you can also list a boat for free and reach travellers planning their trip.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Cadiz and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
